name: Prepositions and Contractions
summary: Portuguese prepositions merge with articles into single contracted words.
explanation:
When a preposition meets a definite article, they usually contract. The key prepositions are de (of/from), em (in/on), a (to), and por (by/through).

De + o becomes do, de + a becomes da; em + o becomes no, em + a becomes na; a + o becomes ao, a + a becomes à. So vou ao mercado (I go to the market), a casa do João (João's house), estou na escola (I'm at school).

These contractions are required, not optional. They also combine with este, esse, and aquele: de + aquele becomes daquele.
commonMistakes: Writing de o or em a separately. They must contract to do and na.
Forgetting the crase à (a + a), used before feminine nouns: vou à praia.
Mixing up de (of/from) and em (in/on), which change the meaning.
Saying 'in Brazil' as em Brasil; it is no Brasil with the article.
Leaving por uncontracted where it should be pelo (por + o) or pela (por + a).
tip: Build a small grid of preposition rows against article columns once; after that the contractions become automatic.
